Page | Zebra Technologies | | | | ## 1. **OVERVIEW** This work instruction describes the procedure of cleaning the ESD workstations. ## 2. **ESD STATIONS CLEANING PROCEDURE** - 1. The ESD station needs to be cleaned daily at the end of every shift. The procedure is included in the daily 5S activity for the verification area. - 2. Start the cleaning by removing the trash, extra supplies, and debris from the tables and the floor. - 3. After removing the trash, empty the trash bins and return them to the designated spots, marked with white tape on the floor. ![](_page_0_Picture_7.jpeg) - 4. Place all the equipment used at the stations to their designated locations (ESD tape, scanners, supplies, vacuum pen, scissors, ESD wrist ties, tape guns). - 5. Refill the supplies at each station if they are low/empty. ![](_page_0_Picture_10.jpeg) | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | | |--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|--------------------|--|--|--| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS

Page | Zebra Technologies | | | | - 6. Refill the printers with media/ribbons if necessary. - 7. Spray and wipe the workstation area with the ESD spray. ![](_page_1_Picture_3.jpeg) 8. Make sure that the vacuum sealer, compressor, and reel counter are powered off. ## 3. **REFERENCES** | Document # | Document Title | |------------|----------------| | N/A | N/A | ## 4. **REVISION HISTORY** The following table lists all revisions (including the original document) to this procedure, the date, and the reason for the revision.
